pr-evidence/engine-probe-serving-profile.gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 found that release evidence could accept a probe whose runtime engine_version did not match the pinned serving profile. The fix now requires engine_version plus serving package/version metadata to match the backend profile; GPT-5.5 follow-up review was clean.
verification: poetry run pytest tests/test_engine_probe.py tests/test_release_evidence.py tests/test_release_bundle.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py -q

python -m py_compile src/restaurant_kv_serving/engine_probe.py src/restaurant_kv_serving/release_evidence.py src/document_kv_cache/engine_probe.py src/restaurant_kv_serving/__init__.py tests/test_engine_probe.py tests/test_release_evidence.py tests/test_release_bundle.py tests/test_public_package.py
GPT-5.5 focused review and clean follow-up review
what_changed: Stamped engine probe records with serving-engine package and version metadata from the backend serving profile.
Exported the new metadata keys through document_kv_cache and restaurant_kv_serving public surfaces.
Made release evidence reject native probes whose serving metadata or runtime engine_version does not match the pinned backend profile.
Updated release bundle and release evidence fixtures plus docs for the stricter release contract.
pr-evidence/engine-probe-serving-profile.why: Release evidence should prove that native vLLM/SGLang connector probes exercised the pinned serving-engine profile instead of accepting arbitrary or stale runtime versions.
